evidence: lib/reddit.ts lines 54-56: USER_AGENT, CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET all read from process.env at module scope — stale snapshots if env vars set after import in tests
lib/reddit.ts line 58: 'let cachedToken: { token: string; expiresAt: number } | null = null' — module-level mutable singleton accumulates state across test runs when module is cached
lib/db.ts: PrismaClient instantiated at module scope using globalForPrisma singleton pattern — this is the canonical Next.js hot-reload fix, not a coupling defect
lib/openrouter.ts: OPENROUTER_API_KEY read inside functions, not at module scope — correct pattern
lib/google-places.ts: GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY read inside function — correct
app/api/scrape/route.ts: INTERNAL_API_TOKEN read inside POST handler — correct
No circular init dependencies detected
No module-level constants computed alongside getter functions where stale snapshot/getter divergence is possible

app/api/scrape/route.ts: 225 LOC with 4 distinct functions (POST handler, fetchWithFallback, enrichAndUpsert, upsertRestaurant); the three helper functions form a coherent pipeline — the orchestration is dense but focused on a single pipeline responsibility
app/api/scrape/route.ts is flagged orphaned by static analysis but is a Next.js HTTP entry point, not a dead file
components/TrendingSection.tsx: geolocation + IP fallback branching creates nested callbacks inside useEffect — complexity is inherent to the async branching required, not structural slop
components/RestaurantCard.tsx: Google Maps URL constructed twice with the same template string at lines 74 and 137 — a named helper or constant would eliminate duplication within the file
Systemic high_cyclomatic_complexity across 9 files: most are API routes with necessary input-validation branching, not a shared design problem requiring systemic intervention
lib/state-machine.ts: isValidTransition and validNextStates are defined but not exported and have no callers — dead code accumulating in the file
lib/validate.ts: 186 LOC, 8 public validators following a consistent pattern — not a design coherence issue despite volume
lib/state-machine.ts: isValidTransition (line 46) and validNextStates (line 51) are private functions with no callers — dead code
app/api/vote/route.ts and app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts: tryTransitionToVerified now wrapped in try-catch with logging — previously flagged issue is FIXED
app/api/vote/route.ts line 27-30: direction already validated as 'up'|'down' before validateVoteInput call at line 58 — the validate call is redundant but not a contract lie

lib/validate.ts interfaces declare all fields as optional (?) but validators throw if fields are absent — the TypeScript type says 'any of these may be absent' but the runtime contract says 'these are required'; callers can pass {} and the compiler will not warn
lib/search.ts groupRestaurantsByName return type is ReturnType<typeof _groupRestaurantsByName> — honest, not a lie
lib/geo.ts extractIp return type string is accurate (always returns a string, falling back to 'unknown')
validateParsedQuery does not validate city field, but city is marked optional in ParsedQuery interface — consistent

lib/search.ts countSearchResults: declared async but doesn't await — returns Promise<number> from prisma.restaurant.count() directly. The async keyword is redundant but not broken; any thrown rejection propagates correctly via the returned promise

app/api/scrape/route.ts fetchWithFallback: receives 'state: ScrapeState' parameter at line 22 but never reads it — the advance callback closes over the outer state variable in POST scope. The returned 'state' field always equals the initial 'PENDING' value passed by the caller
lib/reddit.ts scrapeRedditForRestaurants: results array is written inside a Promise.all with concurrent subreddit processing using push() — safe in Node.js single-threaded event loop but non-obvious
components/TrendingSection.tsx: .catch(() => undefined) silently swallows all fetch errors — intentional UI resilience but hides errors from observability
lib/validate.ts: requireString checks null/undefined then empty-string separately — clear and intentional, not redundant
No identical if/else branches found
No always-true/always-false conditions detected in reviewed files
No dead code after unconditional returns detected

lib/validate.ts: All 8 validator interfaces declare every field as optional (e.g. 'name?: string | null') but the validators throw if fields are absent. TypeScript allows callers to pass {} without compiler error even though runtime will throw — the type contract is weaker than the runtime contract

lib/search.ts line 61: 'restaurants as unknown as GroupableRestaurant[]' double-cast — RestaurantWithRecommendations.status is typed as RestaurantStatus (Prisma enum) while GroupableRestaurant.status is string; the cast hides a real structural divergence
app/api/search-more/route.ts line 25: 'page as unknown as GroupableRestaurant[]' — same double-cast pattern for the same structural mismatch
lib/db.ts line 3: 'globalThis as unknown as { prisma: PrismaClient | undefined }' — canonical Next.js singleton pattern, not a defect
lib/geo.ts line 29: 'return data.city as string' after truthy check — cast is harmless but unnecessary
lib/openrouter.ts: untyped JSON responses cast implicitly — managed via runtime checks before use, acceptable at external API boundaries
No Type:ignore comments found
No Literal aliases duplicating enum values detected
No missing annotations on public API functions

initialization_coupling.dimension_character: The codebase is largely clean on initialization coupling — env var reads are deferred to call time in most modules. The one real concern is lib/reddit.ts, which reads OAuth credentials (CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET) at module scope and caches a token in a module-level mutable variable, creating test-isolation fragility and a boot-order dependency on env vars being set before the module is first imported.
initialization_coupling.score_rationale: One clearly identified defect (module-level env reads + mutable cachedToken in lib/reddit.ts) with test-isolation impact. All other modules follow the correct deferred-read pattern. The db.ts singleton is framework-canonical. Score reflects a single contained issue rather than systemic coupling.

design_coherence.dimension_character: Most files have coherent single responsibilities. The main issues are: dead private functions in lib/state-machine.ts with no callers, a within-file URL construction duplication in RestaurantCard.tsx, and app/api/scrape/route.ts that is densely structured but internally coherent. The systemic high_cyclomatic_complexity across 9 files reflects necessary input-validation branching in API routes rather than design failure.
design_coherence.score_rationale: Design is mostly sound with no god-object or wildly mixed-responsibility files. Dead code in state-machine.ts and intra-file URL duplication in RestaurantCard.tsx are real but local issues. Score is below average because scrape/route.ts mixes HTTP handler concerns with pipeline orchestration logic (enrichAndUpsert, upsertRestaurant) that would be better placed in lib/ for testability.

contract_coherence.dimension_character: The previously open tryTransitionToVerified issue is fixed — both vote routes now wrap the call in try-catch. The remaining open concern is structural: lib/validate.ts declares all input fields optional in its interfaces, but the runtime validators unconditionally throw if those fields are absent, creating a weaker TypeScript contract than the runtime enforcement. Dead private functions in state-machine.ts represent a minor contract-surface accumulation.
contract_coherence.score_rationale: One confirmed resolution (vote try-catch wrap), one new defect (validate interface vs. runtime contract mismatch), dead code in state-machine.ts. No return-type lies, docstring divergences, or getX-mutates-state patterns. Score reflects the interface/runtime mismatch as a meaningful but bounded issue.

logic_clarity.dimension_character: Logic is generally clear and direct. The main clarity issues are: (1) an unused state parameter in fetchWithFallback that suggests stale refactoring — the returned state field is always 'PENDING', (2) async declared on countSearchResults without an await — the async keyword is redundant. No dead code after unconditional returns, no always-true conditions, no identical branches.
logic_clarity.score_rationale: Two minor logic-clarity issues (dead parameter with misleading return value, redundant async) with no functional consequence. No severe flow defects. Score is above average, held back by the dead parameter in a complex orchestration function which increases cognitive load for future maintainers.

type_safety.dimension_character: The most significant type safety issue is that lib/validate.ts declares validator input interfaces with all fields optional, but the validators enforce required-field contracts at runtime. This means TypeScript allows callers to pass empty objects without warning, undermining the pre-write guards on all 5 entity types. The double as-unknown-as casts in lib/search.ts and app/api/search-more/route.ts paper over a real structural divergence between RestaurantStatus (enum) and string in sibling types.
type_safety.score_rationale: Two classes of type safety defects: (1) interface/runtime contract mismatch in validate.ts affecting 8 validators and multiple callers — a subsystem-level issue, and (2) double casts in two files masking a structural type divergence between RestaurantWithRecommendations and GroupableRestaurant. Neither causes runtime failures today, but both remove compiler protection on critical pre-write paths. Score reflects meaningful but not catastrophic weakening of the type contract.

issues.summary: lib/reddit.ts reads OAuth credentials from process.env at module scope, making them stale if env vars are set after import
evidence: Line 54: const USER_AGENT = process.env.REDDIT_USER_AGENT || 'LocalRecos/1.0'
Line 55: const CLIENT_ID = process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_ID
Line 56: const CLIENT_SECRET = process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET
Tests that set process.env.REDDIT_CLIENT_ID after require/import will see undefined in CLIENT_ID
issues.suggestion: Move CLIENT_ID, CLIENT_SECRET reads inside getAccessToken() so they reflect the current env at call time. USER_AGENT can stay as a module constant since it has a safe default.

issues.summary: lib/reddit.ts cachedToken is a module-level mutable singleton that persists across test runs when the module is cached
evidence: Line 58: let cachedToken: { token: string; expiresAt: number } | null = null
cachedToken is written by getAccessToken() and read on subsequent calls — module cache means tests sharing the same process will see a stale token from a previous test
issues.suggestion: This is an acceptable production pattern but tests need isolation. Exporting a resetTokenCacheForTest() function or moving the cache into a closure returned by a factory would allow test isolation without changing the production pattern.

issues.summary: lib/state-machine.ts defines isValidTransition and validNextStates as private functions with no callers
evidence: Line 46: function isValidTransition(from: ScrapeState, to: ScrapeState): boolean — not exported, no callers in codebase
Line 51: function validNextStates(from: ScrapeState): ScrapeState[] — not exported, no callers in codebase
assertValidTransition (line 39) is the only exported function and the only one used in production
issues.suggestion: Delete isValidTransition and validNextStates. They are not referenced anywhere and add dead weight to the file. Re-add with export if they become needed.

issues.summary: components/RestaurantCard.tsx constructs the same Google Maps URL template string twice within the file
evidence: Line 73-75: mapsUrl computed from primaryLocation.address using Google Maps search URL template
Line 137: locMapsUrl computed from loc.address using the same Google Maps search URL template
The string 'https://www.google.com/maps/search/?api=1&query=' appears twice with only the address argument differing
issues.suggestion: Extract a buildMapsUrl(address: string): string helper at module level and call it in both places. Single-file extraction, no new shared module needed.

issues.summary: TrendingSection.tsx geolocation branching has high cyclomatic complexity but it is inherent to the async feature
evidence: useEffect branches across: geolocation available + success, geolocation available + error (IP fallback), geolocation unavailable (IP fallback), then fetchTrending with/without city
The nesting reflects the async branching required by the geolocation API — not an extractable design flaw
fetchIpFallback helper already reduces one level of nesting
issues.suggestion: No change required. The branching is inherent to async geolocation with IP fallback.
issues.reasoning: High cyclomatic complexity is driven by async geolocation branching that cannot be simplified without removing feature behavior. The existing fetchIpFallback extraction already addresses the most extractable part.

issues.summary: app/api/scrape/route.ts is a live Next.js HTTP entry point, not a dead orphan — static analysis misclassifies it
evidence: Static analysis reports: zero importers, 222 LOC
File exports POST handler — called via HTTP by triggerScrape() in app/api/search/route.ts and by SearchPoller component
Next.js API routes are discovered via filesystem routing, not import graph
issues.suggestion: The file is not dead. The design concern about three non-handler functions (fetchWithFallback, enrichAndUpsert, upsertRestaurant) living in a route file rather than lib/ is valid as a pre-existing tracked issue.
issues.reasoning: File is a live Next.js API route entry point discovered via filesystem routing. Zero importers is expected. The design concern about logic in route.ts rather than lib/ is a pre-existing tracked open issue.

issues.summary: High cyclomatic complexity in 9 files is driven by independent input-validation branching, not a shared design pattern requiring systemic fix
evidence: API routes: branches are input validation guards (required fields, enum values, auth token) — each branch is distinct and necessary
lib/hours.ts: parsing complex time format strings requires many branches — inherent to the domain
lib/validate.ts: multiple validators in one file naturally accumulate branches
No shared conditional pattern extractable to a single shared utility across these 9 files
issues.suggestion: No systemic intervention required. Address complexity file-by-file where it has concrete impact on readability or correctness.
issues.reasoning: The high_cyclomatic_complexity pattern across 9 files reflects independent, necessary branching. There is no shared abstraction that would reduce complexity across all 9 files simultaneously. A lint rule raising the threshold would suppress symptoms without improving design.

issues.summary: lib/validate.ts interfaces declare all fields as optional but validators throw if fields are absent — TypeScript type is weaker than the runtime contract
evidence: interface RestaurantInput { name?: string | null; city?: string | null; ... } — all fields optional
validateRestaurantInput calls requireString(o, 'name', 'Restaurant') which throws if name is null/undefined/empty
Caller can pass {} without TypeScript error, but runtime throws ValidationError
Same pattern across all 8 validator functions and their interfaces
issues.suggestion: For fields that validateX enforces as required, remove the '?' optional marker and remove '| null' where null is invalid. Fields that are genuinely optional in the domain should remain optional. This aligns the TypeScript type with the runtime enforcement.

issues.summary: Vote routes call validateVoteInput after already enforcing direction values — validation is redundant after explicit guard
evidence: app/api/vote/route.ts line 27: if (direction !== 'up' && direction !== 'down') return 400
app/api/vote/route.ts line 58: validateVoteInput({ recommendation_id, fingerprint, direction }) — direction already guaranteed to be 'up'|'down' at this point
requirePresent(o, 'direction') in validateVoteInput will never throw because direction is already validated
issues.suggestion: Either remove the explicit guard and let validateVoteInput enforce it, or remove the validateVoteInput call and rely on the explicit guard. The explicit guard produces a cleaner error message and is the preferred approach.

issues.summary: fetchWithFallback in app/api/scrape/route.ts receives a 'state' parameter that is never read and returns a stale value
evidence: Line 22: async function fetchWithFallback(city, query, state: ScrapeState, advance) — state parameter declared
Inside the function, 'state' is never read — all state transitions go through advance() callback which closes over the outer state variable in POST scope
Line 46: return { results, state, empty: false, terms } — returns the parameter value unchanged, which is always 'PENDING' because it was captured by value at call time
The advance() callbacks inside the function mutate the outer scope's state, not this parameter
issues.suggestion: Remove the state parameter from fetchWithFallback. Remove 'state' from its return type. The caller already owns the authoritative state variable via the advance closure.

issues.summary: lib/search.ts countSearchResults is declared async but never uses await — the async keyword is redundant
evidence: Line 154: export async function countSearchResults(city: string, terms: string): Promise<number>
Line 157: return prisma.restaurant.count({ where: buildWhereCondition(normalizedCity, words) }) — returns Promise directly without await
The async keyword adds an implicit promise wrapper around an already-promise-returning expression
issues.suggestion: Remove the async keyword: 'export function countSearchResults(city: string, terms: string): Promise<number>'. This makes the intent explicit — the function returns a Promise from Prisma directly.

issues.summary: lib/validate.ts validator input interfaces declare required fields as optional, undermining TypeScript enforcement on pre-write guards
evidence: interface RestaurantInput { name?: string | null; city?: string | null; status?: string | null; upvotes?: number | null; downvotes?: number | null; ... } — all optional
validateRestaurantInput enforces name, city, status, upvotes, downvotes as required via requireString/requirePresent — but TypeScript does not enforce this
A caller can pass validateRestaurantInput({}) and TypeScript will compile without error, only failing at runtime
Same mismatch in 7 other interfaces: ParsedQuery, RedditPost, ExtractedRestaurant, PlaceDetails, CommunityRecommendationInput, RestaurantVoteInput, VoteInput
issues.suggestion: For fields that validateX enforces as required, remove the '?' optional marker and remove '| null' from the type where null is not a valid value. Fields that are genuinely optional in the data model should remain optional.

issues.summary: RestaurantWithRecommendations.status typed as RestaurantStatus enum but GroupableRestaurant.status typed as string — double cast required at every boundary
evidence: lib/search.ts line 39: status: RestaurantStatus in RestaurantWithRecommendations
lib/restaurant-grouping.ts line 46: status: string in GroupableRestaurant
lib/search.ts line 61: restaurants as unknown as GroupableRestaurant[] — double cast to cross the type boundary
app/api/search-more/route.ts line 25: page as unknown as GroupableRestaurant[] — same double cast
Both casts will silently break if the enum-to-string mismatch ever causes runtime shape differences
issues.suggestion: Change GroupableRestaurant.status from string to RestaurantStatus (importing the enum from @prisma/client). The grouping module has no server-only imports per its file comment and can safely import from @prisma/client. Alternatively change RestaurantWithRecommendations.status to string if the domain logic never needs to discriminate by enum value. Either eliminates both double casts.

add.header: Reddit module reads OAuth credentials at module scope
add.description: lib/reddit.ts captures REDDIT_CLIENT_ID and REDDIT_CLIENT_SECRET at import time into module-level constants, and caches an OAuth token in a mutable module-level variable. Both patterns create test-isolation fragility — env vars set after import are invisible, and the token cache persists across test runs in the same process.

add.header: All other env reads correctly deferred to call time
add.description: OPENROUTER_API_KEY, GOOGLE_PLACES_API_KEY, INTERNAL_API_TOKEN, and DEFAULT_CITY are all read inside functions rather than at module scope. This is the correct pattern and is consistently applied across lib/openrouter.ts, lib/google-places.ts, and all route handlers.
isValidTransition and validNextStates are defined as private (unexported) functions in lib/state-machine.ts with no callers anywhere in the codebase. They are dead code and should be deleted.

add.header: Systemic cyclomatic complexity is independent, not design-level
add.description: High cyclomatic complexity appears in 9 files but each instance reflects necessary input-validation or domain-logic branching. There is no shared extractable abstraction that would address all instances simultaneously. Not a systemic design failure.

add.header: Orphaned file detection misclassifies Next.js API routes
add.description: Static orphan analysis flags all Next.js API route files as having zero importers. These are live HTTP entry points discovered via filesystem routing. The classification is a false positive for this framework.

add.header: tryTransitionToVerified try-catch fix confirmed in both vote routes
add.description: Both app/api/vote/route.ts and app/api/restaurant-vote/route.ts now wrap the tryTransitionToVerified call in try-catch with error logging, preventing a 500 on a committed vote. The previously open issue is resolved.

add.header: Validate interface optionality does not match runtime requirements
add.description: All validator input interfaces in lib/validate.ts mark required fields as optional (?) which allows TypeScript callers to omit them without compiler errors. The runtime validators will throw ValidationError on absent required fields. This is a contract coherence gap — the type says optional, the function enforces required.

add.header: fetchWithFallback state parameter is dead — always returns PENDING
add.description: The state parameter in fetchWithFallback is passed by value and never read inside the function. The returned state field always equals 'PENDING' (the caller's initial value). State transitions go through the advance callback which closes over the outer scope. This is a stale artifact from a prior refactoring.

lib/search.ts countSearchResults is declared async but immediately returns a Prisma promise without awaiting it. The async keyword is redundant and obscures the fact that the function returns Prisma's own Promise directly.

add.header: Validate interfaces weakly typed — all fields optional despite runtime requirements
add.description: lib/validate.ts defines 8 input interfaces with all fields marked optional (?), but the corresponding validators throw if required fields are absent. TypeScript provides no call-site protection. This undermines compile-time safety on pre-write guards for all 5 entity types.

add.header: RestaurantStatus enum vs string divergence causes double casts
add.description: RestaurantWithRecommendations uses the Prisma RestaurantStatus enum for status, while GroupableRestaurant uses plain string. Two files require 'as unknown as GroupableRestaurant[]' double casts to bridge the gap. The structural types are compatible at runtime but the divergence removes compiler protection.
